about.title: About Me
about.description: I'm a Cloud Fullstack Developer focused on building web applications that work in production — not just locally. I work as an IT Intern at Chivas while developing freelance projects with React, Python and AWS. I care about understanding how code behaves under real load, and that shows in every architecture decision I make.

frontend.title: Frontend
frontend.description: I build interfaces with the end user in mind. I focus on reusable components, predictable state and experiences that feel smooth on any device.

backend.title: Backend
backend.description: I design clean, maintainable APIs applying patterns like Singleton, Observer and MVC. I prioritize separation of concerns and correct behavior under load.

cloud.title: Cloud & DevOps
cloud.description: I provision real AWS infrastructure with Terraform and understand it beyond the icons — I know which service to use for each problem, why, and what it costs to get it wrong.

techStack.title: Tech Stack
techStack.subtitle: Technologies I use to build scalable and modern solutions

cloud.title: Cloud & Infrastructure
cloud.subtitle: Services and infrastructure deployed in real client projects.

projects.title: Projects
projects.subtitle: Real applications I've designed, built and deployed

greenpaw.name: Greenpaw
greenpaw.description: Headless storefront built on Shopify with React. The client keeps the Shopify Admin while getting a fully custom frontend experience.
greenpaw.impact: Live store in production with payment processing and custom domain on AWS

flixy.name: Flixy
flixy.description: YouTube-style video platform with HLS streaming, Redis upload queues, S3 storage, Django moderation panel and Power BI analytics.
flixy.impact: Full system with async uploads, content moderation and analytics dashboard

orbit.name: Orbit
orbit.description: Modern landing page with a custom design system in React + MUI and a serverless contact form on AWS — no dedicated backend.
orbit.impact: Live in production at orbit.com.mx with 100% serverless AWS infrastructure

orbit.problem: Orbit needed a modern landing page that clearly communicated its value proposition and handled contact form submissions without relying on a traditional backend server.
orbit.solution: Built a landing page with React, a custom design system with Material UI and animations with Framer Motion. The contact form runs on a serverless AWS architecture with automatic email delivery via Zoho.
decisions: Serverless architecture with AWS Lambda and API Gateway for the contact form — no dedicated server, no fixed infrastructure cost.
Hosting on S3 + CloudFront for static content distribution with low global latency.
Zoho Mail integration for automatic emails when AWS SES production access was unavailable due to domain reputation restrictions.
Terraform to provision and version the entire cloud infrastructure reproducibly.
challenges: Integrating Zoho Mail as an AWS SES alternative when production access was blocked due to domain reputation restrictions.
Configuring API Gateway and Lambda to correctly process and validate contact form requests.
Resolving CORS issues between the frontend and API Gateway in the production environment.
results: Platform live in production at orbit.com.mx.
100% serverless backend with minimal infrastructure maintenance.
Reliable contact form with automatic email notifications.
Bilingual experience with English and Spanish support.

greenpaw.problem: Greenpaw needed an online store that reflected their brand identity beyond what a standard Shopify theme allows, without giving up the Shopify Admin the client already relied on.
greenpaw.solution: Built a headless storefront with React and Material UI connected to the Shopify Storefront API via GraphQL. Shopify remains the commerce engine; the frontend is fully custom.
decisions: Shopify headless instead of a standard theme to keep the client's Admin while having full control over the frontend.
GraphQL via the Shopify Storefront API to fetch product, cart and checkout data flexibly.
Hosting on AWS S3 + CloudFront for scalable static distribution.
Terraform to provision and manage cloud resources consistently.
challenges: Managing cart state in the storefront without a traditional backend session.
Handling the redirect to Shopify checkout while preserving a seamless user experience.
Transferring the domain from Shopify and configuring it in AWS Route 53 with the correct DNS resolution.
results: Branded shopping experience without the limitations of standard themes.
Commerce managed 100% from the Shopify Admin — zero friction for the client.
Hosting and cloud infrastructure managed entirely on AWS.

flixy.problem: A full video platform was needed — with streaming, moderation and analytics — capable of running on a Raspberry Pi with limited hardware resources.
flixy.solution: YouTube-style platform with a plain HTML/CSS/JS frontend, Django backend, Redis upload queues, HLS processing, S3 storage, CloudFront delivery and a Power BI analytics dashboard.
decisions: Django to support authentication, moderation workflows and database-driven features with a mature framework.
Redis queues to control upload concurrency and avoid overloading the Raspberry Pi during HLS processing.
Hard limit of two simultaneous uploads to protect system stability under heavy processing loads.
S3 presigned URLs so clients upload directly to S3, reducing backend load.
CloudFront in front of S3 to improve video delivery performance and optimize transfer costs.
challenges: Processing HLS video on limited Raspberry Pi hardware without the system failing under load.
Designing a queue-based upload flow with Redis to safely handle concurrent requests.
Implementing direct S3 uploads with presigned URLs while keeping the user experience smooth.
Configuring SSL with Certbot and securely exposing the platform from a home-hosted environment.
results: Full video platform with HLS streaming, moderation and analytics.
Moderation panel to approve/reject videos, moderate comments and manage users.
Social features: channel subscriptions, likes/dislikes and per-video comment threads.
Power BI dashboard to visualize platform stats and usage insights.
Backend protected with Redis queues and direct S3 uploads to minimize server load.

tasks: Management and maintenance of IT operations for Estadio Akron and its facilities, ensuring continuous availability of critical systems
Diagnosis and resolution of technical incidents in high-pressure environments — the stadium hosts up to 45,000 people per event, demanding real availability and response time
Direct work with infrastructure under real load, reinforcing my approach of designing systems that hold up when it matters most
Design and development of full web applications for real clients, from architecture to production deployment
Fullstack implementations with React on the frontend and Python/Django on the backend, integrating REST APIs and relational databases
Cloud deployments on AWS with S3, CloudFront, Lambda and API Gateway, with infrastructure managed via Terraform
